title: Number skills
subtitle: The core skills used in every LCA Mathematical Applications question.
curriculumLinks: MCS.2: calculations with positive and negative numbers
MCS.2: order of operations and brackets
MCS.2: convert between fractions, decimals and percentages
examContexts: fractions, decimals and percentages in offers

rules.title: Use BIDMAS for mixed calculations
rules.explanation: When a calculation has different operations, do brackets first, then powers or roots, then division and multiplication from left to right, then addition and subtraction from left to right.
steps: Work out multiplication and division before adding or subtracting.
Write one line of working after each step.

rules.title: Round only at the end
rules.explanation: If a question asks for an answer to the nearest whole number, one decimal place, or two decimal places, keep the full calculator answer until the last line.
rules.formula: Look one digit past the place you need
steps: Find the place value the question asks for.
If the next digit is 5 or more, round up. If it is 4 or less, keep it.

examples.question: Round 18.756 to two decimal places.
examples.working: Two decimal places gives 18.75. The next digit is 6, so round up.

rules.title: Percent means out of 100
rules.explanation: A percentage is a part out of 100. This helps when moving between percentages, decimals and fractions.
steps: To change a percentage to a decimal, divide by 100.
To change a decimal to a percentage, multiply by 100.

rules.title: Check if an answer is sensible
rules.explanation: A fast estimate helps catch calculator mistakes. Round the numbers to easy values and compare your estimate with the exact answer.
steps: Round the numbers to easy values.
If the calculator answer is far away, check the question again.

examples.question: Estimate 19.8 x 5.1.
examples.working: 19.8 is about 20 and 5.1 is about 5. 20 x 5 = 100.
examples.answer: The exact answer should be close to 100.
